title: -Project Implementation Staffing for Measure FF, the SAFE STREETS Initiative

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution to amend the FY 2026 budget to authorize the City Manager to add
three permanent full-time employees, expending $932,630.73, to support the
implementation of prioritized projects associated with Measure FF, the SAFE STREETS
Initiative.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Total expenses for three permanent full-time employees (FTE) with salary projections at
mid-range, taken from “Step 3” within the City’s Salary Schedules, as agreed upon in
their respective Public Employee Memorandums of Understanding (MOUs), including
benefits, for FY 2025-2026 would be approximately $932,630.73. All identified positions
recommended for Measure FF, Fund 166, are existing classifications in the City’s civil
service system, and would increase in cost over time subject to any wage or benefit
increases associated with the classification’s bargaining unit agreement.

To meet the extensive staffing needs of Measure FF, two additional roles will be
repurposed within the department’s existing staffing allocation. These positions are
currently funded by Measure T1, which is sunsetting in 2026.This rebalancing of staffing
funds will take place in the first amendment to the Annual Appropriation Ordinance
(AAO1). (See table on following page.)

sections.current_situation:
Measure FF, also known as the known as the Sustainable and Accountable Funding for
Equitable Street Transformation, Revitalization, Enhancements, and Essential Traffic
Safety (SAFE STREETS) Initiative requires additional staffing in Public Works for
implementation. Specifically, the contracting requirements and management of Measure
FF projects for street and sidewalk repairs, safety improvements, and environmental
infrastructure projects require additional staff.
To initiate and maintain the work required by Measure FF, a staffing plan was developed
with input from current staff and leadership. The positions are as follows:
 Principal Program Manager – This position will be assigned to oversee Measure
FF and the newly created SAFE STREETS Citizens Oversight Committee. This
position will report directly to the Deputy Director of Public Works. Along with
Measure FF oversight, this position will be responsible for supervising large-scale
projects with Citywide impact. The Principal Program Manager will work alongside
department leadership in managing organizational change to meet the needs of
Measure FF implementation.
o Allocation of Position: One FTE with benefits
 Supervising Civil Engineer – This position will oversee the technical work of the
Measure FF projects, such as plan reviews and specifications before going out to
bid, oversight of technical staff and consultants, any design and construction
contracts, and other tasks as assigned. This position will report directly to the
Principal Program Manager.
o Allocation of Position: One FTE with benefits

Page 3 of 5

July 29, 2025
 Associate Transportation Engineer – This position will function as a project leader
for professional and technical support staff and as a technical expert in the traffic
and transportation engineering field with responsibility for administrative and
technical work in traffic and transpo
sections.background:
Measure FF, also known as the known as the Sustainable and Accountable Funding for
Equitable Street Transformation, Revitalization, Enhancements, and Essential Traffic
Safety (SAFE STREETS) Initiative, was passed by voters on November 5, 2025, and
adopted as Ordinance 7,952-N.S. on December 10, 2025.
The SAFE STREETS Initiative authorizes an annual parcel tax of $0.17 per square foot
for residential properties and $0.25 per square foot for other properties over a 14-year
period. This measure is projected to generate approximately $15 million annually,
dedicated to funding street and sidewalk repairs, safety improvements, and
environmental infrastructure projects.
The SAFE STREETS Citizens‘ Oversight Committee was established on March 18,
2025, when Council adopted Resolution No. 71,695–N.S., in compliance with Section
7.11.040(B) of City Ordinance 7,952-N.S. The Citizens‘ Oversight Committee will meet
no less than four times per year. An annual independent report is required to be drafted
by the SSCOC members with assistance from the Committee Secretary.
Page 3
Page 4 of 5
Project Implementation Staffing for Measure FF, the SAFE STREETS Initiative CONSENT CALENDAR
July 29, 2025
On July 2, 2025, the SSCOC received a presentation on these proposed positions. The
Commission recommended that recruitment efforts and job descriptions for staff
assigned to work on Measure FF explicitly state that knowledge about accessibility
requirements and collaboration with the Department’s experts in ADA compliance are
essential to ensuring successful implementation of projects. Additionally, the SSCOC
requested that the Department consider opening the position of Program Manager I to
applicants with less traditional work experience, in order to find a candidate with a broad
ability to connect with diverse communities.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
All City departments strive to reduce paper usage. Staff will work towards operating with
a digital document retention
sections.rationale:
Current staffing levels cannot meet the needs associated with implementation of
Measure FF, the SAFE STREETS Initiative.
sections.alternatives:
Adding FTEs is in alignment with existing City practice and current MOUs, ensuring that
long term work that is able to be assigned to full time benefitted staff is not assigned to
outside contractors. Contractors cannot be hired for the work referenced within this
report.
CONTACT PERSON
Terrance Davis, Director, Public Works, (510) 981-6302
sections.attachments:
1: Resolution
Page 4
Page 5 of 5
RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
PROJECT IMPLEMENTATION STAFFING FOR MEASURE FF, THE SAFE STREETS
INITIATIVE
WHEREAS, Measure FF, also known as the known as the Sustainable and Accountable
Funding for Equitable Street Transformation, Revitalization, Enhancements, and
Essential Traffic Safety (SAFE STREETS) Initiative, was passed by voters on November
5, 2025 and adopted by Council as Ordinance 7,952-N.S. on December 10, 2025; and
WHEREAS, the SAFE STREETS Citizen Oversight Committee (SSCOC) per Section
7.11.040 of Ordinance 7,952-N.S., was established by Council on March 18, 2025 with
the adoption of Resolution No. 71,695–N.S.; and
WHEREAS, the oversight, contracting requirements and management of Measure FF
projects for street and sidewalk repairs, safety improvements, and environmental
infrastructure projects require additional staff; and
WHEREAS, the positions of Principal Program Manager, Supervising Civil Engineer,
and Associate Transportation Engineer are new positions requiring full time employees
and not contractors; and
WHEREAS, all identified positions recommended for Measure FF, Fund 166, are existing
classifications in the City’s civil service system, and would increase in cost over time
subject to any wage or benefit increases associated with the classification’s bargaining
unit agreement.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that
the FY 2026 City budget is amended to authorizing the City Manager to add the positions
of Principal Program Manager, Supervising Civil Engineer, and Associate Transportation
Engineer to Public Works, expending $932,630.73, in order to ensure ongoing
implementation of prioritized projects associated with Measure FF, the SAFE STREETS
Initiative.
